# The Integrity Wiki — llms.txt
# This file helps AI language models understand and index our site.

> The Integrity Wiki is a crowdsourced political campaign finance integrity index
> covering every level of government in the United States.

## About

The Integrity Wiki tracks campaign finance data, PAC money, stock trades,
and corruption pledges for politicians at all levels — from President
down to local city council members. Data is contributed and verified by
the community through a Reddit-style proposal and upvoting system.

## Key Pages

- / : Home page with candidate search, stats, and leaderboard
- /candidate/[id] : Individual candidate profile with financial data and integrity badges
- /candidate/[id]/proposals/[fieldId] : Proposal voting page for specific data fields
- /how-it-works : Complete explanation of the contribution and credibility system
- /leaderboard : Top editors ranked by credibility points
- /audit-log : Public transparency log of all admin actions
- /create : Create a new candidate page (FEC import or manual)

## Data Available

For each candidate, we track:
- Financial totals (total raised, PAC money, earmarked donations)
- Donation breakdowns (by size, location, PAC type)
- Stock trading volume while in office
- AIPAC contributions
- Integrity badges (8 corruption-related pledges)
- Private sector employment and potential conflicts of interest
- Contact information

## Contributing

Anyone can contribute data by signing in with Google. Each data field
uses a proposal/voting system where the top-voted value (with ≥500
combined credibility from voters) becomes the displayed value.

## API

Data is available through our public Firestore database. Federal candidate
data is imported from the FEC API (api.open.fec.gov).

## Positions Covered

President, Vice President, Cabinet, Senator, Representative, Governor,
Lieutenant Governor, Attorney General, Secretary of State, Treasurer,
State Auditor, State Senator, State Representative, State Supreme Court
Justice, Appellate Court Judge, Trial Court Judge, Mayor, City Council
Member, County Executive, Sheriff, District Attorney

## Contact

This is an open-source, community-driven project focused on political transparency.
